summary.intent: Address Codex automated review feedback on PR #683 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically fixing IME (Input Method Editor) composition handling bugs in the Editable component.
summary.outcome: Three rounds of Codex review feedback were addressed across two fix commits. The IME fixes (suppress-next-enter guard, blur/submit no-ops during composition) passed all 46 unit tests plus spec-conformance checks, with clippy and fmt clean. The commit was pushed and Codex threads resolved.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask spec validate` to enforce spec-conformance between spec/components/input/editable.md and implementation
CI pipeline is multi-stage: formatting → snapshots → clippy → workspace check → unit tests → adapter/DOM browser tests → coverage → feature-flag groups → release verification
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` comment and tracked by reaction emoji (`eyes` = in progress) and unresolved thread count via GitHub GraphQL API
The `snapshot-reviewed` label must be re-applied after each fix push to signal human snapshot approval to CI
IME composition state requires a `suppress_next_enter` flag to prevent the Enter keypress that confirms a composition from also triggering form submit

code.finding: CompositionEnd handling was missing a guard for the 'composition just ended' state, causing the confirming Enter keystroke to fall through to submit/blur handlers
Submit and blur event handlers needed explicit early-return guards when is_composing is true, preventing stale pre-composition text from committing
Spec needed updating to document CompositionConfirmKey suppression behavior alongside is_composing state transitions

workflow: Polling loop pattern: `sleep 120 && gh api graphql ... | jq` to check Codex thread count + CI checks in a single command reduces context switches
GitHub GraphQL is required to resolve review threads (REST API cannot resolve threads); `resolveReviewThread` mutation takes a thread node ID
Replying to a thread comment (`in_reply_to`) before resolving it leaves a clear audit trail of what was fixed in which commit
Running `cargo llvm-cov` scoped to the changed module (`-- input::editable`) is faster than full coverage and sufficient for per-PR review
Nightly fmt can diverge from stable fmt on test assertion formatting; always run `cargo +nightly fmt --all` before the check, not after

friction: Codex posted three separate review rounds requiring repeated push/resolve/re-trigger cycles, each with a ~2 minute polling wait
The `snapshot-reviewed` label had to be manually re-applied after each push, creating a potential for CI to stall if forgotten
Nightly fmt produced a formatting change not caught by the pre-commit check, requiring an extra commit fixup before the final push
GitHub GraphQL thread IDs (PRRT_* node IDs) are not surfaced in standard PR views and require a separate GraphQL query to obtain before resolving
open_items: Coverage report for the IME path was generated but not evaluated against a threshold — actual coverage numbers for the new composition guard code are not verified
The remaining CI jobs (coverage, feature-flag groups, release verification) were still pending at the time of the final commit push
Whether the third Codex pass will open additional threads on the IME changes is unknown — a fourth review cycle may be needed
